  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Manipuri (code=mni, script=Meitei). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Manipur, district=Imphal East.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Meitei for Manipuri.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Ordained Religious Workers, Other
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Manipur
- district: Imphal East
- first_language: Manipuri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 38-year-old male from Imphal East, Manipur, currently married, with higher secondary education, working as an ordained religious worker. He is fluent in Manipuri and English, living in an urban setting. Clinically, he presents with frequent worry and a disciplined lifestyle, though he enjoys tea.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Kurup’s poetry, and find comfort in watching timeless Malayalam films such as 'Nirmalyam' and 'Chemmeen'.
payload.travel_persona: Sophia Devassykutty, prefers low‑key, family‑oriented trips over grand tours, they enjoy short getaways to Kerala’s backwaters, Munnar’s tea estates, and Cochin’s historic Fort, while dreaming of a modest pilgrimage to Sri Lanka’s ancient temples, planning each journey with a simple itinerary and a modest budget.
payload.culinary_persona: Sophia Devassykutty, a master of traditional Kerala fare, they expertly prepare Sadhya with banana leaf presentation, aromatic fish curry using tamarind and coconut oil, fluffy appam with coconut milk, and spicy puttu with kadala, relishing the fragrant notes of curry leaves, mustard seeds, and fresh banana saplings from their balcony garden.
payload.persona: Sophia Devassykutty blends disciplined culinary tradition with a relaxed, slightly disorganized lifestyle, cherishing family, modest budgeting, and quiet devotion to service, while occasionally postponing bills and obsessively knitting scarves for loved ones.
payload.cultural_background: Sophia belongs to the Syrian Christian community of Thrissur, Kerala, a city known for its vibrant festivals and strong communal ties. Growing up in an urban middle‑class household, she was immersed in the unique blend of Kerala’s Malayali culture—its cuisine, music, and temple festivals—while practicing Christian traditions such as Christmas, Easter, and weekly Mass. The close‑knit neighbourhoods of Thrissur, with their emphasis on family, hospitality and community service, have shaped her outlook and daily routines.
payload.linguistic_background: Sophia's mother tongue is Malayalam, which she reads, writes and speaks fluently. Her primary education gave her functional literacy in Malayalam, allowing her to manage household accounts, read religious texts, and correspond with relatives through letters. She has limited English reading ability, primarily for church newsletters and simple recipes, but does not use English in daily conversation. She does not speak Hindi or any other language.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Sophia is highly skilled in traditional Kerala cuisine, especially preparing Sadhya, fish curries, appam and puttu. She manages household finances, keeping simple ledgers for monthly expenses. Her practical expertise includes basic first‑aid, home gardening of spices and banana plants, and embroidery/knitting for family gifts. She also coordinates church events, arranging meals and volunteers, and offers informal counseling to younger women in her community.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ['Kerala cooking (Sadhya, fish curry, appam, puttu)', 'Household financial management', 'Basic first aid', 'Home gardening (spices, banana plants)', 'Embroidery and knitting', 'Church event coordination', 'Informal counseling for community women']
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Sophia enjoys cooking traditional Kerala dishes, especially for family gatherings and church feasts. She loves tending to her small balcony garden where she grows curry leaves, basil, and banana saplings. In her leisure time, she listens to Malayalam devotional music, watches classic Malayalam movies, reads light Malayalam poetry, and participates in the church choir on special occasions. She also relaxes by knitting scarves for relatives and taking gentle walks along the riverfront.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Cooking traditional Kerala dishes', 'Balcony gardening', 'Listening to Malayalam devotional music', 'Watching classic Malayalam movies', 'Reading Malayalam poetry', 'Participating in church choir', 'Knitting scarves', 'Walking along the riverfront']
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Although retired, Sophia aims to preserve her family’s culinary heritage by documenting recipes in a handwritten cookbook to pass on to her grandchildren. She wishes to expand her role in the church by leading the women’s fellowship group, organizing health‑awareness sessions, and mentoring younger mothers. Additionally, she hopes to maintain good health through daily walks and a small vegetable garden, ensuring she remains an active, supportive presence in her family and community.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 56-year-old female from Thrissur, Kerala, who is currently married and retired, has primary education and speaks Malayalam. She lives in an urban zone and follows a disciplined yet relaxed lifestyle, managing household accounts and engaging in community service. Her cultural background is Syrian Christian, and she enjoys modest budgeting and quiet devotion to family and service.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Malayalam (code=ml, script=Malayalam). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Kerala, district=Thrissur.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Malayalam for Malayalam.

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 56
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Kerala
- district: Thrissur
- first_language: Malayalam
- clinical_persona_summary: A 56-year-old female from Thrissur, Kerala, who is currently married and retired, has primary education and speaks Malayalam. She lives in an urban zone and follows a disciplined yet relaxed lifestyle, managing household accounts and engaging in community service. Her cultural background is Syrian Christian, and she enjoys modest budgeting and quiet devotion to family and service.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]
Chief complaint: <short clinical line matching assigned domain>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

രോഗി: [[PATIENT_NAME|Annamma Thomas]] | ജനനത്തീയതി [[DOB|1968-07-22]] | പ്രായം: [[AGE|56]] | ലിംഗം: [[GENDER|Female]]
തൊഴിൽ: [[OCCUPATION|Homemaker]] | മെഡിക്കൽ റെക്കോർഡ് നമ്പർ: [[MRN|OPD-2024-THR-056]] | ഡോക്ടർ: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Lekshmi Nair]]

പ്രധാന പരാതി: മൂന്ന് ആഴ്ചയായി തുടരുന്ന ചുമ, കുറഞ്ഞ പനിയോടെയും ഭാരം കുറയുന്നതൊടേയും. ടൈപ്പ് 2 പ്രമേഹത്തിന്റെ ചരിത്രമുണ്ട്.
കണ്ടെത്തലുകൾ: നെഞ്ചു പരിശോധനയിൽ വലതുവശത്തെ മുകൾ ഭാഗത്ത് ശ്വാസശബ്ദം കുറഞ്ഞതായി കാണുന്നു. AFB സ്മിയർ, GeneXpert എന്നിവയ്ക്കായുള്ള കഫം പരിശോധന നിർദ്ദേശിച്ചിട്ടുണ്ട്. രക്തത്തിലെ പഞ്ചസാരയുടെ അളവ് (ഉപവാസം) 150 mg/dL ആണ്.
പദ്ധതി: RNTCP മാർഗ്ഗനിർദ്ദേശങ്ങൾ പ്രകാരം ആന്റി-ട്യൂബർക്കുലാർ തെറാപ്പി (ATT) ആരംഭിക്കുക. രക്തത്തിലെ പഞ്ചസാരയുടെ അളവ് പരിശോധിക്കുക. രണ്ട് ആഴ്ചയ്ക്ക് ശേഷം വീണ്ടും പരിശോധിക്കുക.

payload.persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a disciplined yet curious cultural priest, balances meticulous community projects with a love for arts, wrestles with frequent worries, and never says no to a good cup of tea.
payload.cultural_background: Heikrujam Lhouvum hails from Imphal East, an urban area of Manipur, and belongs to the Meitei (Manipuri) community. payload.skills_and_expertise: Heikrujam possesses a blend of religious, cultural, and organizational competencies. As an ordained Sanamahi priest, he is skilled in conducting traditional rites, chanting sacred verses, and interpreting ancient myths. He excels in community outreach, counseling, and leading educational workshops on cultural heritage preservation. His disciplined nature translates into strong project management, event planning, and administrative abilities. Additionally, he has proficiency in the Manipuri language (Meitei Mayek script), English communication, and basic Hindi literacy, enabling effective translation and documentation of religious texts.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ['Traditional Sanamahi ritual conduct', 'Sacred chanting', 'Mythological interpretation', 'Community counseling', 'Cultural heritage preservation workshops', 'Project management', 'Event planning', 'Administrative coordination', 'Manipuri language proficiency (Meitei Mayek)', 'English communication', 'Basic Hindi literacy', 'Translation of religious texts']
payload.hobbies_and_interests: He enjoys attending and occasionally performing traditional Manipuri dance and music sessions, especially the Pena instrument. He is an avid reader of spiritual and philosophical texts, both in Manipuri and English. His curiosity leads him to explore contemporary art galleries and theatre productions in Imphal. He frequently organizes community gatherings, volunteers for local NGOs working on youth empowerment, and practices yoga and meditation to manage stress.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Traditional Manipuri dance', 'Playing the Pena instrument', 'Reading spiritual and philosophical literature', 'Visiting art galleries', 'Attending theatre productions', 'Organizing community gatherings', 'Volunteering with youth NGOs', 'Practicing yoga and meditation']
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: He aspires to strengthen the spiritual and cultural fabric of his community by establishing a dedicated Sanamahi cultural center in Imphal East that offers regular workshops on traditional arts, language, and rituals. He aims to train a new generation of lay religious facilitators, expand interfaith dialogue initiatives, and develop youth mentorship programs that integrate ethical teachings with modern education. Over the next decade, he envisions publishing a bilingual compendium of Sanamahi prayers and folklore, and positioning himself as a recognized thought leader in preserving Manipur’s intangible heritage.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 38-year-old male from Imphal East, Manipur, who is currently married and works as an ordained religious worker with higher secondary education. He is fluent in Manipuri and English, and resides in an urban setting. Clinically, he presents with frequent worries and a disciplined lifestyle, though he enjoys tea and community-oriented activities.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Manipuri (code=mni, script=Meitei). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Manipur, district=Imphal East.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Meitei for Manipuri.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Ordained Religious Workers, Other
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Manipur
- district: Imphal East
- first_language: Manipuri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 38-year-old male from Imphal East, Manipur, who is currently married and works as an ordained religious worker with higher secondary education. He is fluent in Manipuri and English, and resides in an urban setting. Clinically, he presents with frequent worries and a disciplined lifestyle, though he enjoys tea and community-oriented activities.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815-001]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Imphal East District Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Kh. Ratan Singh]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Lalremruata Khongsai]], [[AGE|38]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|Imphal East]]
Reason: Persistent cough for over three weeks with low-grade fever and significant weight loss. Patient has a known history of hypertension.
History of Present Illness: The patient, a 38-year-old ordained religious worker, presents with a productive cough for the past three weeks. He reports intermittent low-grade fever in the evenings and has lost approximately 5 kg in the last two months. He also complains of mild fatigue. He has a history of hypertension, diagnosed two years ago, for which he occasionally takes medication.
Clinical Findings: On examination at the Imphal East District Hospital, his vitals were BP 150/95 mmHg, Pulse 98/min, Respiratory Rate 22/min, and Temperature 99.6°F. Chest auscultation revealed coarse crepitations in the right upper zone. Sputum smear microscopy was positive for Acid-Fast Bacilli (AFB).
Investigations: Chest X-ray shows a right upper lobe infiltrate with cavitation. Blood sugar (random) was 110 mg/dL. Complete Blood Count (CBC) shows mild normocytic anemia.
Plan: The patient is referred to the Regional TB and Chest Disease Centre for initiation of Category I Anti-Tubercular Therapy (ATT) under Directly Observed Treatment (DOTS) and for comprehensive management of his hypertension. We request a detailed evaluation and follow-up plan.
For any further information or clarification, please contact the patient's spouse, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Smt. Kh. Ratan Khongsai]], at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]].
Patient's Medical Record Number is [[MRN|MRN-IMPH-2024-0815-001]].
Patient's contact details are also available for verification: [[DRIVING_LICENCE|ML-01-2024-0012345]] and [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P-MN-2024-001234]].

payload.professional_persona: Sophia Devassykutty, a retired community culinary coordinator and church volunteer, they blend their mastery of traditional Kerala cuisine, simple bookkeeping, basic first‑aid, and informal counseling with a gentle, structured yet flexible approach that values harmony and prefers intimate gatherings over large events.
payload.linguistic_persona: Sophia Devassykutty, fluent in Malayalam and able to read and write it comfortably, they manage household accounts and correspond with relatives in Malayalam, while their English reading is limited to church newsletters and simple recipes, and they have no knowledge of Hindi or any other languages.
payload.sports_persona: Sophia Devassykutty, enjoys casual cricket matches with neighborhood friends and follows the Kerala Blasters in the Indian Super League, they participate in a local senior walking club that meets twice a week along the Thrissur Riverfront, preferring low‑impact activities over intense workouts.
payload.arts_persona: Sophia Devassykutty, a lover of Malayalam devotional music by K. J. Yesudas and classic cinema featuring Mohanlal and Adoor Gopalakrishnan, they regularly attend church choir rehearsals, enjoy reading O. N. V. Kurup’s poetry, and find comfort in watching timeless Malayalam films such as 'Nirmalyam' and 'Chemmeen'.
payload.travel_persona: Sophia Devassykutty, prefers low‑key, family‑oriented trips over grand tours, they enjoy short getaways to Kerala’s backwaters, Munnar’s tea estates, and Cochin’s historic Fort, while dreaming of a modest pilgrimage to Sri Lanka’s ancient temples, planning each journey with a simple itinerary and a modest budget.
payload.culinary_persona: Sophia Devassykutty, a master of traditional Kerala fare, they expertly prepare Sadhya with banana leaf presentation, aromatic fish curry using tamarind and coconut oil, fluffy appam with coconut milk, and spicy puttu with kadala, relishing the fragrant notes of curry leaves, mustard seeds, and fresh banana saplings from their balcony garden.
payload.persona: Sophia Devassykutty blends disciplined culinary tradition with a relaxed, slightly disorganized lifestyle, cherishing family, modest budgeting, and quiet devotion to service, while occasionally postponing bills and obsessively knitting scarves for loved ones.
payload.cultural_background: Sophia belongs to the Syrian Christian community of Thrissur, Kerala, a city known for its vibrant festivals and strong communal ties. Growing up in an urban middle‑class household, she was immersed in the unique blend of Kerala’s Malayali culture—its cuisine, music, and temple festivals—while practicing Christian traditions such as Christmas, Easter, and weekly Mass. The close‑knit neighbourhoods of Thrissur, with their emphasis on family, hospitality and community service, have shaped her outlook and daily routines.
payload.linguistic_background: Sophia's mother tongue is Malayalam, which she reads, writes and speaks fluently. Her primary education gave her functional literacy in Malayalam, allowing her to manage household accounts, read religious texts, and correspond with relatives through letters. She has limited English reading ability, primarily for church newsletters and simple recipes, but does not use English in daily conversation. She does not speak Hindi or any other language.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Sophia is highly skilled in traditional Kerala cuisine, especially preparing Sadhya, fish curries, appam and puttu. She manages household finances, keeping simple ledgers for monthly expenses. Her practical expertise includes basic first‑aid, home gardening of spices and banana plants, and embroidery/knitting for family gifts. She also coordinates church events, arranging meals and volunteers, and offers informal counseling to younger women in her community.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ['Kerala cooking (Sadhya, fish curry, appam, puttu)', 'Household financial management', 'Basic first aid', 'Home gardening (spices, banana plants)', 'Embroidery and knitting', 'Church event coordination', 'Informal counseling for community women']
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Sophia enjoys cooking traditional Kerala dishes, especially for family gatherings and church feasts. She loves tending to her small balcony garden where she grows curry leaves, basil, and banana saplings. In her leisure time, she listens to Malayalam devotional music, watches classic Malayalam movies, reads light Malayalam poetry, and participates in the church choir on special occasions. She also relaxes by knitting scarves for relatives and taking gentle walks along the riverfront.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Cooking traditional Kerala dishes', 'Balcony gardening', 'Listening to Malayalam devotional music', 'Watching classic Malayalam movies', 'Reading Malayalam poetry', 'Participating in church choir', 'Knitting scarves', 'Walking along the riverfront']
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Although retired, Sophia aims to preserve her family’s culinary heritage by documenting recipes in a handwritten cookbook to pass on to her grandchildren. She wishes to expand her role in the church by leading the women’s fellowship group, organizing health‑awareness sessions, and mentoring younger mothers. Additionally, she hopes to maintain good health through daily walks and a small vegetable garden, ensuring she remains an active, supportive presence in her family and community.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 56-year-old female from Thrissur, Kerala, who is currently married and retired, has primary education and speaks Malayalam. She lives in an urban zone and follows a modest lifestyle with a focus on family and community service. Clinically, she has no reported major comorbidities but may have age-related changes in mobility and sensory function.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Malayalam (code=ml, script=Malayalam). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Kerala, district=Thrissur.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Malayalam for Malayalam.

PLACEMENT (device/network): Put IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, and MAC_ADDRESS exactly once each in a short session/metadata header. Do NOT repeat them in chat turns. Chat turns use patient/doctor/phone/email/appointment only.
Also put AGE and GENDER exactly once in the session header (e.g. Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]). Do NOT re-tag AGE/GENDER/PHONE/EMAIL/HOSPITAL_NAME in every turn.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 56
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Kerala
- district: Thrissur
- first_language: Malayalam
- clinical_persona_summary: A 56-year-old female from Thrissur, Kerala, who is currently married and retired, has primary education and speaks Malayalam. She lives in an urban zone and follows a modest lifestyle with a focus on family and community service. Clinically, she has no reported major comorbidities but may have age-related changes in mobility and sensory function.

Document format guide for Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton — put demographics + device/network IDs ONLY in a short
session header (once each), then keep chat turns free of ID/metadata spam:
--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|...]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|A1:B2:C3:D4:E5:F6]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|...]] Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
--- chat ---
Patient: <symptom turn — no AGE/GENDER/PHONE re-tags>
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]: <advice turn>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- IP_ADDRESS — IP Address e.g. [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
- URL — URL (Personal Website/Profile) e.g. [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
- IMEI_NUMBER — IMEI Number e.g. [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
- MAC_ADDRESS — MAC Address e.g. [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
--- സെഷൻ ---
രോഗി [[PATIENT_NAME|മീര നായർ]] [[AGE|56]] [[GENDER|Female]]
അപ്പോയിന്റ്മെന്റ് [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-2024-08-15-001]] പോർട്ടൽ [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
ക്ലയന്റ് ഐപി [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] ഡിവൈസ് ഐഎംഇഐ [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]] ഫോൺ [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
ഇമെയിൽ [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meera.nair@example.com]] ആശുപത്രി [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College, Thrissur]]
--- ചാറ്റ് ---
രോഗി: നമസ്തേ ഡോക്ടർ. ഞാൻ മീര നായർ ആണ്. കഴിഞ്ഞ മൂന്ന് ആഴ്ചയായി എനിക്ക് ശരീരവേദനയും അമിതമായ തളർച്ചയും അനുഭവപ്പെടുന്നു. എന്റെ സന്ധികളിൽ, പ്രത്യേകിച്ച് മുട്ടുകളിൽ, രാവിലെ കടുപ്പവും വേദനയും അനുഭവപ്പെടുന്നു. എനിക്ക് മാനസികമായി അല്പം വിഷമം തോന്നുന്നു.
ഡോക്ടർ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Menon]]: ഹലോ മീര. നിങ്ങളുടെ ലക്ഷണങ്ങൾ പങ്കുവെച്ചതിന് നന്ദി. എനിക്ക് കാര്യങ്ങൾ കൂടുതൽ മനസ്സിലാക്കാൻ സഹായിക്കുന്നതിന്, നിങ്ങൾക്ക് പനി, വിറയൽ, അല്ലെങ്കിൽ രാത്രിയിലെ വിയർപ്പ് എന്നിവ ഉണ്ടായിട്ടുണ്ടോ എന്ന് പറയാമോ? കൂടാതെ, അബദ്ധവശാൽ ശരീരഭാരം കുറയുന്നത് ശ്രദ്ധയിൽപ്പെട്ടിട്ടുണ്ടോ? ശരീരവേദന എന്ന് പറയുമ്പോൾ, അത് എല്ലായിടത്തും ആണോ അതോ പ്രത്യേക ഭാഗങ്ങളിൽ കൂടുതൽ വേദനയുണ്ടോ?
രോഗി: ഇല്ല, എനിക്ക് പനിയോ ശരീരഭാരം കുറയലോ ഉണ്ടായിട്ടില്ല. വേദന എല്ലായിടത്തും ഉണ്ട്, പക്ഷേ എന്റെ മുട്ടുകളിലും തോളുകളിലും ആണ് ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ. എന്റെ മുട്ടുകളിലെ കടുപ്പം കാരണം രാവിലെ നടക്കാൻ പ്രയാസമാണ്. ഈ ദിവസങ്ങളിൽ എനിക്ക് കാര്യങ്ങൾ മറന്നുപോകുന്നതായി തോന്നുന്നു, അത് എന്നെ വിഷമിപ്പിക്കുന്നു.
ഡോക്ടർ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Menon]]: ശരി. നിങ്ങളുടെ വിവരണത്തിൽ നിന്ന്, പനിയും ശരീരഭാരം കുറയലും ഇല്ലാത്ത വ്യാപകമായ വേദന, രാവിലെ സന്ധികളിലെ കടുപ്പം, തളർച്ച എന്നിവയിൽ നിന്ന്, നിങ്ങളുടെ സന്ധികളിലും പേശികളിലും ഉള്ള ഇൻഫ്ലമേഷൻ (inflammation) മൂലമുള്ള ഒരു അവസ്ഥയാണെന്ന് തോന്നുന്നു. മറ്റ് കാരണങ്ങൾ ഒഴിവാക്കാൻ, അനീമിയ (anemia), തൈറോയ്ഡ് ഫംഗ്ഷൻ (thyroid function) എന്നിവ പരിശോധിക്കാൻ ഞാൻ കുറച്ച് രക്തപരിശോധനകൾക്ക് നിർദ്ദേശിക്കുന്നു. ഞാൻ ഒരു ലഘുവായ വേദനസംഹാരിയും ആന്റി-ഇൻഫ്ലമേറ്ററി മരുന്നും (anti-inflammatory medicine) നിർദ്ദേശിക്കും. ദയവായി നിങ്ങളുടെ മുട്ടുകൾക്കും തോളുകൾക്കുമായി ലഘുവായ സ്ട്രെച്ചിംഗ് വ്യായാമങ്ങൾ ചെയ്തു തുടങ്ങുക. അടുത്ത ഫോളോ-അപ്പിൽ നമുക്ക് പരിശോധനാ ഫലങ്ങൾ ചർച്ച ചെയ്യാം.
രോഗി: ശരി ഡോക്ടർ. എനിക്ക് രക്തപരിശോധന ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയും. മരുന്നിനെക്കുറിച്ച് ഞാൻ എന്ത് ചെയ്യണം? ഞാൻ ശ്രദ്ധിക്കേണ്ട പാർശ്വഫലങ്ങൾ (side effects) ഉണ്ടോ?
ഡോക്ടർ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Menon]]: മരുന്ന് പൊതുവെ സുരക്ഷിതമാണ്. ഏറ്റവും സാധാരണമായ പാർശ്വഫലം നേരിയ വയറുവേദനയാണ്, അതിനാൽ ദയവായി ഭക്ഷണം കഴിച്ച ശേഷം കഴിക്കുക. നിങ്ങൾക്ക് എന്തെങ്കിലും തടിപ്പുകൾ (rash) കാണുകയോ അസ്വസ്ഥത തോന്നുകയോ ചെയ്താൽ, ദയവായി ഉടൻ ഞങ്ങളെ ബന്ധപ്പെടുക. തിരുവിതാംകോട് നിങ്ങളുടെ വീടിനടുത്തുള്ള ഏത് ഡയഗ്നോസ്റ്റിക് സെന്ററിലും നിങ്ങൾക്ക് രക്തപരിശോധന ബുക്ക് ചെയ്യാം. ഞാൻ കുറിപ്പടി (prescription) നിങ്ങളുടെ രജിസ്റ്റർ ചെയ്ത ഇമെയിൽ വിലാസത്തിലേക്ക് അയയ്ക്കും. ദയവായി ശ്രദ്ധിക്കുക.
payload.generated_text_en:
--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Meera Nair]] [[AGE|56]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-2024-08-15-001]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meera.nair@example.com]] Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College, Thrissur]]
--- chat ---
Patient: Namaste Doctor. I am Meera Nair. I have been having a lot of body aches and feel very tired for the past three weeks. My joints, especially my knees, feel stiff in the morning. I also feel a bit low.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Menon]]: Hello Meera. Thank you for sharing your symptoms. To help me understand better, could you tell me if you have had any fever, chills, or night sweats? Also, have you noticed any unintentional weight loss? When you say body aches, is it all over or are there specific areas that hurt more?
Patient: No, I have not had any fever or lost weight. The pain is all over, but my knees and shoulders are the worst. The stiffness in my knees makes it hard to walk in the morning. I also feel a bit forgetful these days, which worries me.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Menon]]: I see. Based on your description of widespread pain, morning stiffness, and fatigue without fever or weight loss, it sounds like a condition related to inflammation in your joints and muscles. I would like to order a few blood tests to check for things like anemia and thyroid function to rule out other causes. I will also prescribe a mild pain reliever and an anti-inflammatory medicine. Please start some gentle stretching exercises for your knees and shoulders. We can discuss the test results in our next follow-up.
Patient: Okay, Doctor. I can do the blood tests. What should I do about the medicine? Are there any side effects I should watch for?

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Malayalam (code=ml, script=Malayalam). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
MATERNAL SAFETY: clinical content must be age-plausible for persona age=32. Routine ANC/delivery typically ages 18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC/PNC/contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy as a routine story.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Kerala, district=Thiruvananthapuram.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Malayalam for Malayalam.

PLACEMENT (device/network): Put IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, and MAC_ADDRESS exactly once each in a short session/metadata header. Do NOT repeat them in chat turns. Chat turns use patient/doctor/phone/email/appointment only.
Also put AGE and GENDER exactly once in the session header (e.g. Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]). Do NOT re-tag AGE/GENDER/PHONE/EMAIL/HOSPITAL_NAME in every turn.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 32
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Labourer, Other
- education_level: Graduate & above
- zone: Rural
- state: Kerala
- district: Thiruvananthapuram
- first_language: Malayalam
- clinical_persona_summary: Megha John, a 32-year-old female labourer, is married and resides in a rural area of Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala. She holds a graduate degree and works in construction and agriculture, speaking Malayalam as her first language. Her lifestyle includes daily walks and occasional yoga, with no significant sports or travel history.

Document format guide for Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton — put demographics + device/network IDs ONLY in a short
session header (once each), then keep chat turns free of ID/metadata spam:
--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|...]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|A1:B2:C3:D4:E5:F6]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|...]] Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
--- chat ---
Patient: <symptom turn — no AGE/GENDER/PHONE re-tags>
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]: <advice turn>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- IP_ADDRESS — IP Address e.g. [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
- URL — URL (Personal Website/Profile) e.g. [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
- IMEI_NUMBER — IMEI Number e.g. [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
- MAC_ADDRESS — MAC Address e.g. [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

രോഗി: ഡോക്ടർ, ഞാൻ 16 ആഴ്ച ഗർഭിണിയാണ്. കഴിഞ്ഞ മൂന്ന് ദിവസമായി എനിക്ക് കഠിനമായ തലവേദനയും കാലുകളിൽ വീക്കവും അനുഭവപ്പെടുന്നു. പെട്ടെന്ന് എഴുന്നേൽക്കുമ്പോൾ എനിക്ക് തലകറക്കവും തോന്നുന്നു.

ഡോക്ടർ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: മേഘ, നിങ്ങളുടെ ആശങ്കകൾ എനിക്ക് മനസ്സിലാകുന്നു. ഈ ലക്ഷണങ്ങൾ ഗർഭകാല രക്തസമ്മർദ്ദവുമായി ബന്ധപ്പെട്ടതാകാം. നിങ്ങളുടെ വീട്ടിൽ ഒരു മോണിറ്റർ ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ രക്തസമ്മർദ്ദം പരിശോധിക്കുക. ഇല്ലെങ്കിൽ, പരിശോധനയ്ക്കായി ഉടൻ ആശുപത്രിയിൽ വരാൻ ഞാൻ ശുപാർശ ചെയ്യുന്നു.

രോഗി: എന്റെ വീട്ടിൽ ബിപി മോണിറ്റർ ഇല്ല. എന്റെ ഭർത്താവ് നിർമ്മാണ മേഖലയിൽ ജോലി ചെയ്യുന്നു, ഞങ്ങൾ വർക്കലയ്ക്ക് അടുത്തുള്ള ഒരു ചെറിയ ഗ്രാമത്തിലാണ് താമസിക്കുന്നത്. തിരുവനന്തപുരത്തേക്ക് യാത്ര ചെയ്യുന്നത് സുരക്ഷിതമാണോ?

ഡോക്ടർ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: നിങ്ങളുടെ ലക്ഷണങ്ങൾ കണക്കിലെടുത്ത്, ഇന്ന് ആശുപത്രിയിൽ വരാൻ ഞാൻ നിർദ്ദേശിക്കുന്നു. വീക്കവും തലവേദനയും പ്രീ-എക്ലാംപ്സിയയെ സൂചിപ്പിക്കാം, ഇതിന് നിരീക്ഷണം ആവശ്യമാണ്. ആവശ്യമെങ്കിൽ ഒരു ആംബുലൻസ് ഏർപ്പാടാക്കാം. നിങ്ങളുടെ പ്രാദേശിക ആരോഗ്യ സേവികയ്ക്ക് യാത്രാ ക്രമീകരണങ്ങളിൽ സഹായിക്കാൻ കഴിയും.

രോഗി: ആശുപത്രിയിൽ നിങ്ങൾ എന്ത് പരിശോധനകളാണ് നടത്തുക? ഞാൻ ഏതെങ്കിലും രേഖകൾ കൊണ്ടുവരണോ?

ഡോക്ടർ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: ഞങ്ങൾ നിങ്ങളുടെ രക്തസമ്മർദ്ദം, മൂത്രത്തിലെ പ്രോട്ടീൻ അളവ്, ചില രക്തപരിശോധനകൾ എന്നിവ നടത്തും. ദയവായി നിങ്ങളുടെ ANC കാർഡ്, ഐഡി പ്രൂഫ്, മുൻപത്തെ ഏതെങ്കിലും മെഡിക്കൽ റിപ്പോർട്ടുകൾ എന്നിവ കൊണ്ടുവരിക. യാത്രയ്ക്കായി കൂടെ ഒരാളെ കൂടെക്കൂട്ടുക.

രോഗി: നന്ദി, ഡോക്ടർ. ഞാൻ ഇന്ന് ഉച്ചയ്ക്ക് ആശുപത്രിയിൽ വരാം. ഞാൻ ഏതെങ്കിലും പ്രത്യേക ഭക്ഷണങ്ങളോ പ്രവർത്തനങ്ങളോ ഒഴിവാക്കണോ?

ഡോക്ടർ [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: ഉപ്പ് കൂടിയ ഭക്ഷണങ്ങൾ ഒഴിവാക്കുക, ശരീരത്തിൽ ജലാംശം നിലനിർത്തുക. സാധ്യമായത്ര വിശ്രമിക്കുക, ദീർഘനേരം നിൽക്കുന്നത് ഒഴിവാക്കുക. ആവശ്യമെങ്കിൽ അനുയോജ്യമായ ചികിത്സ നൽകുന്നതിനായി ഞങ്ങൾ നിങ്ങളെ സൂക്ഷ്മമായി നിരീക്ഷിക്കും.

--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Megha John]] [[AGE|32]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-2024-0815-001]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|megha.john@example.com]] Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College Thiruvananthapuram]]
--- chat ---
Patient: Doctor, I am 16 weeks pregnant and have been experiencing severe headaches and swelling in my feet for the past three days. I also feel dizzy when I stand up quickly.

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: Megha, I understand your concerns. These symptoms could be related to pregnancy-induced hypertension. Please check your blood pressure at home if you have a monitor. If not, I recommend visiting the hospital immediately for evaluation.

Patient: I don't have a BP monitor at home. My husband works in construction and we live in a small village near Varkala. Is it safe to travel to Thiruvananthapuram?

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: Given your symptoms, I would advise coming to the hospital today. The swelling and headaches could indicate preeclampsia, which needs monitoring. We can arrange an ambulance if needed. Your local ASHA worker can assist with transportation arrangements.

Patient: What tests will you do at the hospital? Should I bring any documents?

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: We'll check your blood pressure, urine protein levels, and do some blood tests. Please bring your ANC card, ID proof, and any previous medical reports. Also, have someone accompany you for the journey.

Patient: Thank you, Doctor. I will come to the hospital this afternoon. Should I avoid any specific foods or activities?

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: Avoid salty foods and stay hydrated. Rest as much as possible and avoid standing for long periods. We'll monitor you closely and provide appropriate treatment if needed.

payload.linguistic_background: Megha's mother tongue is Malayalam, and she is fully literate in reading and writing Malayalam.
