She is adept at organising community tasks, coordinating groups for festivals and local development projects, and maintains basic bookkeeping for small co‑ops. payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete ER Triage & Nursing Notes for clinical domain Psychiatry / Behavioral Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Malayalam (code=ml, script=Malayalam). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Psychiatry / Behavioral Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Kerala, district=Thiruvananthapuram.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Malayalam for Malayalam.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once in the arrival/RTA/ambulance line. Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 32
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Labourer, Other
- education_level: Graduate & above
- zone: Rural
- state: Kerala
- district: Thiruvananthapuram
- first_language: Malayalam
- clinical_persona_summary: Megha John is a 32-year-old female labourer from rural Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, who is married and holds a graduate degree. She speaks Malayalam as her first language and has functional English proficiency. Her lifestyle includes daily walks and occasional yoga, with no significant sports or travel history.

Document format guide for ER Triage & Nursing Notes (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|...]]
Arrived by ambulance / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|...]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Vitals / acuity: <short notes>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

വൈറ്റൽസ് / തീവ്രത: BP 120/80, HR 88, RR 18, SpO2 98% മുറിയിലെ വായുവിൽ. രോഗി ഉത്കണ്ഠാകുലനാണ്, കണ്ണീരൊഴുകുന്നു, എന്നാൽ സഹകരിക്കുന്നു. ജാഗരൂകനും ദിശാബോധമുള്ളവനും x4.

നിലവിലെ രോഗചരിത്രം: രോഗി തിരുവനന്തപുരത്ത് നിന്നുള്ള 32 വയസ്സുള്ള ഒരു വനിതാ തൊഴിലാളിയാണ്, ഭർത്താവ് കൊണ്ടുവന്നു. കഴിഞ്ഞ ഒരാഴ്ചയായി വർദ്ധിച്ചുവരുന്ന ഉത്കണ്ഠയും പാനിക് അറ്റാക്കുകളും റിപ്പോർട്ട് ചെയ്യുന്നു, സമീപകാലത്തെ കുടുംബ സമ്മർദ്ദങ്ങൾ കാരണം ഉണ്ടായത്. പ്രത്യേക പദ്ധതികളൊന്നുമില്ലാതെ പാസീവ് ആത്മഹത്യാ ചിന്തകൾ പ്രകടിപ്പിക്കുന്നു, താൻ ഒരു ഭാരമാണെന്ന് പ്രസ്താവിക്കുന്നു. ഹാലുസിനേഷനുകളോ മിഥ്യാധാരണകളോ നിഷേധിക്കുന്നു. സമീപകാലത്തെ ലഹരി ഉപയോഗം നിഷേധിക്കുന്നു.
നഴ്സിംഗ് വിലയിരുത്തൽ: രോഗി ജാഗരൂകനാണ്, ദിശാബോധമുള്ളവനാണ്, താൻ പറഞ്ഞ പ്രായമായി കാണപ്പെടുന്നു. ഭാവം ഉത്കണ്ഠാകുലവും വിഷാദപരവുമാണ്. സംസാരത്തിന് സാധാരണ വേഗതയും താളവുമുണ്ട്. ചിന്താപ്രക്രിയ യുക്തിസഹവും ലക്ഷ്യബോധമുള്ളതുമാണ്. നിലവിലെ വൈകാരികാവസ്ഥ കാരണം വിധിനിർണ്ണയം തകരാറിലാണ്. സുരക്ഷാ വിലയിരുത്തൽ സൂചിപ്പിക്കുന്നത് പാസീവ് ആത്മഹത്യാ ചിന്തകൾ, ഉദ്ദേശ്യമോ പദ്ധതിയോ ഇല്ല. രോഗിയെ 1:1 ആത്മഹത്യാ മുൻകരുതലുകളിൽ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തിയിരിക്കുന്നു.
പദ്ധതി: സൈക്യാട്രിക് ഒബ്സർവേഷൻ യൂണിറ്റിലേക്ക് പ്രവേശിപ്പിക്കുക. 1:1 നിരീക്ഷണം തുടരുക. ഡോ. അഞ്ജലി നായർ വഴി സൈക്യാട്രിക് വിലയിരുത്തൽ ആരംഭിക്കുക. ഉത്കണ്ഠയ്ക്ക് കുറഞ്ഞ അളവിൽ ലോറാസെപാം 0.5mg PO q6h PRN ആരംഭിക്കുക. ശാന്തവും കുറഞ്ഞ ഉത്തേജനവുമുള്ള ഒരു അന്തരീക്ഷം നൽകുക. കുടുംബത്തെ അറിയിക്കുകയും സൈക്യാട്രിസ്റ്റിനൊപ്പം കുടുംബ യോഗം ക്രമീകരിക്കുകയും ചെയ്യുക.
payload.generated_text_en:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College, Thiruvananthapuram]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0815-001]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Megha John]] [[AGE|32]] [[GENDER|Female]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|01]]
Arrived by ambulance / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|KL01AB1234]] (exactly once).
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|John Varghese]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]
Vitals / acuity: BP 120/80, HR 88, RR 18, SpO2 98% on room air. Patient is anxious, tearful, but cooperative. Alert and oriented x4.
Chief Complaint: "I feel like I can't breathe and I don't want to be here anymore."
History of Present Illness: Patient is a 32-year-old female labourer from Thiruvananthapuram, brought in by her husband. She reports escalating anxiety and panic attacks over the past week, triggered by recent family stressors. She endorses passive suicidal ideation without a specific plan, stating she feels like a burden. Denies hallucinations or delusions. Denies recent substance use.
Nursing Assessment: Patient is alert, oriented, and appears her stated age. Affect is anxious and dysphoric. Speech is of normal rate and rhythm. Thought process is logical and goal-directed. Judgment is impaired due to current emotional state. Safety assessment indicates passive suicidal ideation, no intent or plan. Patient is placed on 1:1 suicide precautions.
Plan: Admit to psychiatric observation unit. Continue 1:1 observation. Initiate psychiatric evaluation by Dr. Anjali Nair. Start low-dose Lorazepam 0.5mg PO q6h PRN for anxiety. Provide a calm, low-stimulus environment. Inform family and arrange for family meeting with the psychiatrist.

S. Chithra.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]

  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]

  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Surgical / Operative Note for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Malayalam (code=ml, script=Malayalam). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Kerala, district=Thiruvananthapuram.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Malayalam for Malayalam.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 32
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Labourer, Other
- education_level: Graduate & above
- zone: Rural
- state: Kerala
- district: Thiruvananthapuram
- first_language: Malayalam
- clinical_persona_summary: Megha John is a 32-year-old female labourer from Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, who is married and holds a graduate degree. She speaks Malayalam and functional English, works in construction and agriculture, and resides in a rural area. Her lifestyle includes daily walks and occasional yoga, with no significant sports or travel history.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the

payload.professional_persona: Dorje Bodhi, a seasoned call‑centre operator with decades of experience, combines a calm, empathetic voice, sharp active‑listening, and meticulous data‑entry skills to resolve caller issues efficiently while quietly mentoring newer agents, reflecting his methodical reliability and deep compassion.
payload.linguistic_persona: Dorje Bodhi, whose mother tongue is Marathi, navigates daily conversations fluently while relying on colleagues for any written tasks, as he remains illiterate in Marathi, Hindi, and English yet adept at spoken communication and visual computer cues.
payload.sports_persona: Dorje Bodhi enjoys morning walks through the countryside, follows cricket passionately, cheering for the Mumbai Indians, and occasionally joins the village’s informal kabaddi matches, though his age limits intense play.
payload.arts_persona: Dorje Bodhi is an avid fan of Marathi folk music, regularly listening to Avadhoot Gupte’s bhavgeet and the classic tunes of Lata Mangeshkar, and he delights in watching Tamasha performances featuring shahir Shahir Sable, often humming the melodies afterwards.
payload.travel_persona: Dorje Bodhi prefers carefully planned short trips with his wife and grandchildren, cherishing visits to nearby pilgrimage sites like Shirdi and Ajanta, the coastal breezes of Goa, and occasional pilgrimages to Bodh Gaya, while dreaming of a serene retreat in the Himalayan foothills.
payload.culinary_persona: Dorje Bodhi delights in savoring traditional Marathi dishes such as Puran Poli, Misal Pav, and Solkadhi, prefers fresh garden herbs like coriander and fenugreek for his tea, and while he enjoys buying street‑food snacks like vada pav, he rarely cooks because he relies on his wife’s kitchen.
payload.persona: Dorje Bodhi is a compassionate, methodical call‑centre veteran who balances frugality, community devotion, and a love for simple village pleasures, though he sometimes worries over health and finances.
payload.cultural_background: Dorje Bodhi belongs to a close‑knit rural Marathi community in Hingoli, Maharashtra. The agrarian rhythms, seasonal festivals, and the spirit of ‘jugaad’ shape his everyday life. As a convert to Navayana (Ambedkarite) Buddhism, his cultural identity intertwines traditional Maharashtrian customs—such as Ganesh Chaturthi, folk music, and communal meals—with Buddhist values of compassion, equality, and meditation. The village’s oral storytelling traditions and the collective harmony of its residents reinforce his compassionate and cooperative nature.
payload.linguistic_background: Dorje’s mother tongue is Marathi, which he speaks fluently and uses for all personal and work interactions. Because he never received formal schooling, he is illiterate in Marathi, Hindi, and English, relying on oral communication and on colleagues for any written tasks. He has no functional knowledge of English or any other language. In the call centre, he primarily handles Marathi‑speaking customers, using spoken language and basic computer navigation guided by visual cues.
payload.skills_and_expertise: With decades of experience as a call‑centre operator, Dorje has honed strong customer‑service skills, including calm voice handling, active listening, and rapid problem identification. His methodical approach ensures accurate data entry and adherence to scripts, while his empathetic nature aids in conflict resolution and de‑escalation of upset callers. He is proficient in basic computer operations, call‑logging software, and time‑management techniques. His collaborative spirit makes him an effective team player and mentor for newer agents.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Customer service", "Phone communication", "Active listening", "Problem solving", "Empathy", "Organizational planning", "Basic computer operation", "Time management", "Team coordination", "Conflict resolution"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Outside of work, Dorje enjoys listening to Buddhist talks and chants, attending village community gatherings, and walking through the countryside after his shift. He finds peace in gardening small pots of vegetables and herbs, and often plays card games like ‘Teen Patti’ with friends at the local tea stall. He loves Marathi folk music and occasionally joins local Tamasha performances as an enthusiastic audience member. Regular meditation sessions help him manage stress and maintain his compassionate outlook.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Buddhist talks and chants", "Attending community gatherings", "Walking in the countryside", "Gardening", "Playing card games (Teen Patti)", "Listening to Marathi folk music", "Enjoying local Tamasha performances", "Meditation"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Dorje wishes to remain a dependable presence in his call‑centre for the next few years, gradually taking on a mentorship role where he can share his extensive experience with younger agents. He aims to secure a pension and sufficient savings to support his wife and grandchildren, while also planning a modest post‑retirement venture such as a small vegetable stall. Spiritually, he aspires to deepen his meditation practice and occasionally lead brief meditation sessions for community members, contributing to local welfare initiatives that promote health, education, and social harmony.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Automated SMS Notifications for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=West Bengal, district=Jalpaiguri.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Nepali.

LENGTH HARD LIMIT: entire SMS ≤ ~500 characters, but you MUST still include every mandatory entity tag below at least once (compact tags OK).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 48
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Foresters and Related Workers, Other
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: West Bengal
- district: Jalpaiguri
- first_language: Nepali
- clinical_persona_summary: Geeta Das is a 48-year-old female forest worker in rural West Bengal, currently married, with an illiterate education level. She is Nepali-speaking and resides in Jalpaiguri district, where she works as a forester and lives in a rural community. Her lifestyle includes cash-only spending, modest gold savings, and functional comprehension of Hindi and Bengali.

Document format guide for Automated SMS Notifications (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
CRITICAL: keep the WHOLE SMS under ~400 characters / 2–4 sentences. Not a form dump.
MUST tag [[APPOINTMENT_ID|…]] whenever an appointment is mentioned.
Example:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]: appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC Kanpur]] on 21-May 10:30 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sharma]]. Confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]. MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-001]].

MANDATORY inline entities (6 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text: [[PATIENT_NAME|रमेश शर्मा]]: तपाईँको oncology follow-up [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] मा छ। [[HOSPITAL_NAME|काठमाण्डौ अस्पताल]] मा [[DOCTOR_NAME|डा. अनिता खड्का]] सँग। कृपया तपाईँको [[MRN|MRN-WB-2024-001]] ल्याउनुहोस्। [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] मा confirm गर्न कल गर्नुहोस्।
payload.generated_text_en: [[PATIENT_NAME|Geeta Das]]: Your oncology follow-up is on [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jalpaiguri District Hospital]] with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Ananda Roy]]. Please bring your [[MRN|MRN-WB-2024-001]]. Call [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] to confirm.

payload.professional_persona: Shibani Das, a 22‑year‑old aspiring home‑based caterer and community organizer, blends their practical household management skills with a curiosity for new recipes, balancing meticulous planning for festive menus with the flexibility to improvise during Bihu events, and they are assertively building a small business while staying reliable to their family responsibilities.
payload.linguistic_persona: Shibani Das, fluent in Assamese and functional in Hindi with only occasional English exposure, comfortably switches between the lyrical flow of Assamese at home, Hindi for school communications, and the bits of English they pick up from movies, using their curiosity to expand their vocabulary whenever possible.
payload.sports_persona: Shibani Das, a casual sports enthusiast, enjoys early morning jogs along the Brahmaputra riverbank, follows the IPL fervently cheering for the Delhi Capitals, supports the Assam football team in local league matches, and occasionally joins a weekend yoga class at the community centre.
payload.arts_persona: Shibani Das, an avid listener of Assamese folk tunes, especially the soulful voice of Zubeen Garg and the energetic Bihu drums, often practices Bihu dance routines, delights in short stories by Mamoni Raisom Goswami, and occasionally watches Hindi cinema featuring stars like Ranveer Singh, which fuels their creative spirit.
payload.travel_persona: Shibani Das, a spontaneous traveler who loves day‑trips to Kaziranga National Park for elephant sightings and weekend getaways to Shillong for its cool climate, prefers budgeting travel through train tickets and shared buses, but dreams of a future backpacking tour of Southeast Asia, especially Thailand, while balancing family expectations.
payload.culinary_persona: Shibani Das, a proficient home chef, delights in preparing Assamese specialties like pitha, tenga, masor tenga, and bamboo shoot pickle, often seasoning with mustard oil, khar, and fresh herbs like coriander and mint, while occasionally exploring Hindi street food such as aloo chaat during weekend market visits.
payload.persona: Shibani Das, a 22‑year‑old dreamer who juggles school, a budding catering business, and Bihu festivities with disciplined budgeting, occasional midnight cravings for spicy snacks, and a habit of replaying folk tunes while stirring a pot.
payload.cultural_background: Shibani Das belongs to the Assamese community of northeastern India. Growing up in the urban town of Tinsukia, she has been immersed in the region's tea‑garden culture, the vibrant celebrations of Bihu, and the everyday rhythms of a Hindu household that blends traditional rituals with modern aspirations.
payload.linguistic_background: Shibani's mother tongue is Assamese, which she reads and writes fluently in the Assamese script. She also has functional proficiency in Hindi, both spoken and written, having learned it through formal schooling and daily communication. Her primary education gave her basic literacy in both languages, while her exposure to English is limited to occasional interactions and media consumption.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Shibani is proficient in managing a household, including meal planning, traditional Assamese cooking, budgeting, and childcare. She has strong practical skills in preparing festive dishes such as pitha, tenga, and various fish preparations. She also handles basic digital tasks like using a smartphone for communication, online grocery shopping, and staying connected with family through messaging apps. Additionally, she assists in organizing community events, especially during Bihu, and has basic knowledge of Assamese folk dance.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Household Management","Traditional Assamese Cooking","Budgeting","Childcare","Smartphone Use","Community Event Coordination","Basic Assamese Folk Dance"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Shibani enjoys listening to Assamese folk music, dancing Bihu, experimenting with new recipes, and reading short stories in the Assamese language. She likes gardening, especially growing herbs and small vegetables for kitchen use. On weekends, she often visits local markets, watches Hindi movies, and spends time with friends at tea stalls, balancing social interaction with quiet moments of reading or knitting.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Assamese Folk Music","Bihu Dance","Cooking and Recipe Experimentation","Reading Assamese Short Stories","Gardening (Herbs & Vegetables)","Visiting Local Markets","Watching Hindi Movies","Knitting"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Shibani aims to further her education by completing her secondary schooling while simultaneously launching a small home‑based catering venture that offers authentic Assamese festive meals. She hopes to become a recognized community organizer for cultural events, especially Bihu celebrations, and mentor other young women in her neighbourhood to pursue education and self‑reliance. Ultimately, she envisions her family’s financial stability improving through her culinary business, enabling her to support her husband’s aspirations and provide quality education for future children.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 22‑year‑old female from Assam, currently married, with primary education and living in an urban area. She speaks Assamese as her first language and has functional Hindi proficiency. Her lifestyle includes managing a home‑based catering business and engaging in Bihu cultural activities.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:

ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
MATERNAL SAFETY: clinical content must be age-plausible for persona age=22. Routine ANC/delivery typically ages 18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC/PNC/contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy as a routine story.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Assam, district=Tinsukia.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Bengali for Assamese.

- sex / gender: Female
- age: 22
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Assam
- district: Tinsukia
- first_language: Assamese
- clinical_persona_summary: A 22‑year‑old female from Assam, currently married, with primary education and living in an urban area. She speaks Assamese as her first language and has functional Hindi proficiency. Her lifestyle includes managing a home‑based catering business and engaging in Bihu cultural activities.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:

- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]

- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.

প্ৰক্ৰিয়া: জৰুৰীকালীন চিজেৰিয়ান ডেলিভাৰী।

ইণ্ডিকেচন: ৰোগী এগৰাকী 22 বছৰীয়া G1P0, গৰ্ভধাৰণৰ 38 সপ্তাহত ভ্ৰূণৰ গতিবিধি কমি যোৱাৰ বাবে ভৰ্তি কৰোৱা হৈছে। প্ৰাৰম্ভিক নন-ষ্ট্ৰেছ পৰীক্ষাৰ ফলাফল অ-সক্ৰিয় আছিল। পৰীক্ষাৰ সময়ত, তেওঁৰ 4 চেণ্টিমিটাৰ চাৰ্ভিকেল ডাইলেচনৰ সৈতে প্ৰাৰম্ভিক প্ৰসৱ বেদনা হৈছিল। অবিৰতভাৱে ভ্ৰূণ নিৰীক্ষণত পুনৰাবৃত্তি হোৱা লেট ডিচেলৰেচন দেখা গৈছিল। জৰুৰীকালীন চিজেৰিয়ান ডেলিভাৰী কৰাৰ সিদ্ধান্ত লোৱা হ’ল।

বিৱৰণ: ৰোগীজনক অপাৰেচন থিয়েটাৰলৈ লৈ যোৱা হ’ল আৰু স্পাইনেল এনেস্থেচিয়া দিয়া হ’ল। এটা ফানেনষ্টেইল incision কৰা হ’ল, আৰু এটা নিম্ন ট্ৰান্সভাৰ্ছ ইউটেৰাইন incision কৰা হ’ল। 3.2 কেজি ওজনৰ এটা ল’ৰা সন্তান জন্ম হ’ল, যাৰ এপগাৰ স্ক’ৰ 1 মিনিটত 7 আৰু 5 মিনিটত 9 আছিল। প্লাচেন্টা স্বতঃস্ফূৰ্তভাৱে ওলাই আহিল। আনুমানিকভাৱে 250 মিলিলিটাৰ তেজৰ ক্ষতি হৈছিল। জৰায়ু ভালদৰে সংকুচিত হৈছিল, আৰু ৰক্তক্ষৰণ বন্ধ কৰা হৈছিল। ইউটেৰাইন incision দুটা স্তৰত বন্ধ কৰা হ’ল, আৰু পেটৰ বেৰখন সাধাৰণভাৱে বন্ধ কৰা হ’ল।

পোষ্ট-অপাৰেটিভ পাঠ্যক্ৰম: ৰোগীয়ে প্ৰক্ৰিয়াটো ভালদৰে সহ্য কৰিলে আৰু স্থিতিশীল অৱস্থাত পোষ্ট-এনেস্থেচিয়া কেয়াৰ ইউনিটলৈ স্থানান্তৰ কৰা হ’ল। শিশুটি নিৰীক্ষণৰ বাবে নিওনেটাল ইনটেনচিভ কেয়াৰ ইউনিটলৈ স্থানান্তৰ কৰা হ’ল।

পৰিকল্পনা: ভাইটাল চাইনসমূহ নিৰীক্ষণ কৰি থকাটো অব্যাহত ৰাখিব। প্ৰয়োজন অনুসৰি আইভি পেৰাচিটামল আৰু আইবুপ্ৰফেনৰ সৈতে বিষনাশক ঔষধ দিব। সোনকালে খোজ কাঢ়িবলৈ উৎসাহিত কৰিব। সহ্য কৰিব পৰা ধৰণে মুখৰ ভিতৰত খাদ্য আৰম্ভ কৰিব। থ্ৰম্বো প্ৰোফিল্যাক্সিস অব্যাহত ৰাখিব। প্ৰসৱোত্তৰ ৰক্তক্ষৰণৰ সাৱধানতা অৱলম্বন কৰা হ’ল। [[RELATIVE_NAME|Manik Gogoi]]ক প্ৰক্ৰিয়াটো আৰু মাতৃ আৰু শিশু উভয়ৰে অৱস্থাৰ বিষয়ে অৱগত কৰোৱা হ’ল।
payload.generated_text_en:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Tinsukia Civil Hospital]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT-1]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|15]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Rina Gogoi]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Arun Sharma]]
[[PATIENT_ID|PID-TNS-7890]] [[MRN|MRN-TNS-2024-001]]

Procedure: Emergency Cesarean delivery.

Indication: Patient is a 22-year-old G1P0 at 38 weeks gestation, admitted for decreased fetal movements. Initial non-stress test was non-reactive. On examination, she was in early labor with cervical dilation of 4 cm. Continuous fetal monitoring showed recurrent late decelerations. Decision was made for an emergency Cesarean delivery.

Description: The patient was taken to the operating room and induced with spinal anesthesia. A Pfannenstiel incision was made, and a low transverse uterine incision was performed. A male infant, weighing 3.2 kg, was delivered with Apgar scores of 7 at 1 minute and 9 at 5 minutes. The placenta was delivered spontaneously. Estimated blood loss was 250 mL. The uterus was well-contracted, and hemostasis was achieved. The uterine incision was closed in two layers, and the abdominal wall was closed in the standard fashion.

Post-operative course: The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the post-anesthesia care unit in stable condition. The infant was transferred to the neonatal intensive care unit for observation.

Plan: Continue monitoring of vital signs. Analgesia with IV paracetamol and ibuprofen as needed. Encourage early ambulation. Start oral diet as tolerated. Continue thromboprophylaxis. Postpartum hemorrhage precautions in place. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Manik Gogoi]] was informed of the procedure and the status of both mother and infant.

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Hingoli.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Marathi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 67
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Operator, Call Centre
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Hingoli
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 67-year-old male operator in a call centre, currently married, with no formal education and living in a rural area of Maharashtra. His first language is Marathi, and he is illiterate in all languages. He is concerned about his health and finances.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
