Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Punjab, district=Jalandhar.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Gurmukhi for Punjabi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 29
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Punjab
- district: Jalandhar
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 29-year-old female from Jalandhar, Punjab, never married, with below-primary education and an urban residence. She speaks Punjabi and Hindi, and her lifestyle includes daily walks, yoga, and home-based tiffin catering. Clinically, she may exhibit stress-related over-thinking and a disciplined approach to budgeting and meal planning.

Document format guide for Lab Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (English pivot; TYPE names stay ASCII forever):
Lab report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Barddhaman District Hospital]] District [[DISTRICT|Purba Bardhaman]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-WB-2024-0815]] Patient ID [[PATIENT_ID|PID-WB-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] [[AGE|27]] [[GENDER|Female]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
Ordered by [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Riya Banerjee]]
Results table (analyte values NOT tagged as PHI entities).
NEVER write [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] / [[এম.আর.এন|…]] — only PATIENT_NAME, MRN, etc.

MANDATORY inline entities (9 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ਰਿਪੋਰਟ ਦੀ ਮਿਤੀ: 15 ਅਗਸਤ 2024

ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਆਮ ਥਕਾਵਟ ਅਤੇ ਤਣਾਅ ਦੀਆਂ ਸ਼ਿਕਾਇਤਾਂ ਨਾਲ ਪੇਸ਼ ਹੁੰਦਾ ਹੈ। ਨਿਯਮਤ ਸਿਹਤ ਜਾਂਚ ਅਤੇ ਮੈਟਾਬੋਲਿਕ ਪੈਨਲ ਲਈ ਆਰਡਰ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ।

ਟੈਸਟ ਦੇ ਨਤੀਜੇ:
ਪੈਰਾਮੀਟਰ                    | ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਦਾ ਨਤੀਜਾ | ਸੰਦਰਭ ਸੀਮਾ
----------------------------|----------------|------------------
ਹੀਮੋਗਲੋਬਿਨ (g/dL)           | 10.8           | 12.0 - 15.5
RBC ਕਾਊਂਟ (10^6/cumm)       | 3.9            | 4.2 - 5.4

ਪਲੇਟਲੈਟਸ (10^3/cumm)       | 250            | 150 - 450
ਫਾਸਟਿੰਗ ਬਲੱਡ ਗਲੂਕੋਜ਼ (mg/dL)| 95             | 70 - 100
ਪੋਸਟ-ਪ੍ਰੈਂਡੀਅਲ ਗਲੂਕੋਜ਼ (mg/dL)| 145            | <140
ਕੁੱਲ ਕੋਲੇਸਟ੍ਰੋਲ (mg/dL)   | 198            | <200
LDL ਕੋਲੇਸਟ੍ਰੋਲ (mg/dL)     | 125            | <100
HDL ਕੋਲੇਸਟ੍ਰੋਲ (mg/dL)     | 42             | >40
ਟ੍ਰਾਈਗਲਿਸਰਾਈਡਸ (mg/dL)       | 165            | <150
SGOT (U/L)                  | 48             | 7 - 35
SGPT (U/L)                  | 55             | 7 - 35
ਸੀਰਮ ਕ੍ਰੀਏਟੀਨਾਈਨ (mg/dL)    | 0.9            | 0.6 - 1.1
ਯੂਰੀਆ (mg/dL)                | 22             | 7 - 20

ਵਿਆਖਿਆ:
ਨਤੀਜੇ ਸੁਝਾਅ ਦਿੰਦੇ ਹਨ ਕਿ ਹਲਕਾ ਨੋਰਮੋਸਾਈਟਿਕ ਅਨੀਮੀਆ ਅਤੇ ਜਿਗਰ ਦੇ ਐਨਜ਼ਾਈਮਾਂ (SGOT/SGPT) ਵਿੱਚ ਮਾਮੂਲੀ ਵਾਧਾ ਹੈ, ਜੋ ਸੰਭਵ ਤੌਰ 'ਤੇ ਪੋਸ਼ਣ ਦੀ ਕਮੀ ਅਤੇ ਤਣਾਅ ਕਾਰਨ ਹੈ। ਫਾਸਟਿੰਗ ਅਤੇ ਪੋਸਟ-ਪ੍ਰੈਂਡੀਅਲ ਗਲੂਕੋਜ਼ ਸਵੀਕਾਰਯੋਗ ਸੀਮਾਵਾਂ ਦੇ ਅੰਦਰ ਹਨ। ਲਿਪਿਡ ਪ੍ਰੋਫਾਈਲ ਵਿੱਚ LDL ਅਤੇ ਟ੍ਰਾਈਗਲਿਸਰਾਈਡਸ ਸੀਮਾ 'ਤੇ ਉੱਚੇ ਦਿਖਾਈ ਦਿੰਦੇ ਹਨ। ਲੋਹੇ ਨਾਲ ਭਰਪੂਰ ਭੋਜਨ ਅਤੇ ਤਣਾਅ ਪ੍ਰਬੰਧਨ ਤਕਨੀਕਾਂ 'ਤੇ ਧਿਆਨ ਕੇਂਦਰਿਤ ਕਰਦੇ ਹੋਏ ਖੁਰਾਕ ਸੰਬੰਧੀ ਸਲਾਹ ਦੀ ਸਿਫਾਰਸ਼ ਕੀਤੀ ਜਾਂਦੀ ਹੈ। ਹੀਮੋਗਲੋਬਿਨ ਅਤੇ ਜਿਗਰ ਦੇ ਕਾਰਜ ਟੈਸਟਾਂ ਦਾ ਮੁੜ-ਮੁਲਾਂਕਣ ਕਰਨ ਲਈ 3 ਮਹੀਨਿਆਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਫਾਲੋ-ਅਪ ਕਰੋ।

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

- sex / gender: Male
- age: 34
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Mechanic, Dairy Maintenance
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Urban
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Chennai
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: Mr. Mahavir Jain, 34, is a married mechanic in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, with middle education and Tamil as his first language. He works in dairy maintenance and is currently in an urban zone. His lifestyle includes daily walks along Marina Beach and weekend badminton.

Beneficiary [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|26]] / [[GENDER|Female]]
ASHA: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|...]] | Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Visit findings: <2-4 short sentences>

- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.

payload.professional_persona: Mahavir Jain, a seasoned mechanic with over ten years of hands‑on experience across motor vehicles, agricultural gear and dairy processing equipment, excels at fault diagnosis, cost‑effective improvisation (jugaad), and they are now eyeing senior technician certification in advanced diesel diagnostics while dreaming of a modest workshop that blends functional design with aesthetic tool storage.
payload.linguistic_persona: Mahavir Jain, a native Tamil speaker who reads and writes fluently in Tamil, uses functional English for work manuals and everyday communication, and they have limited exposure to Hindi, mainly through occasional public signage.
payload.sports_persona: Mahavir Jain follows the Chennai Super Kings fervently, plays weekend badminton at a nearby community center, and they maintain their fitness with daily morning walks along Marina Beach and occasional cricket batting practice.
payload.arts_persona: Mahavir Jain enjoys listening to the soulful renditions of M.S. Subbulakshmi and the compositions of Ilaiyaraaja, watches classic Tamil films starring Rajinikanth and Kamal Haasan, and spends evenings sketching minimalist line drawings of temples and machinery, finding inspiration in the aesthetic harmony of both.
payload.travel_persona: Mahavir Jain prefers budget-friendly road and train trips, has explored Pondicherry’s French quarter, trekked the misty hills of Kodaikanal, and they dream of witnessing the sunrise at Rishikesh, balancing their love for solitude with occasional family outings.
payload.culinary_persona: Mahavir Jain favors simple South‑Indian vegetarian fare such as idli, dosa with coconut chutney, sambar, rasam, and curd rice, relishes the seasonal mangoes and enjoys occasional sweet treats like Mysore Pak, but rarely cooks at home, preferring freshly prepared meals from trusted neighborhood stalls.
payload.persona: Mahavir Jain is a methodical, compassionate mechanic who merges his love for art and non‑violence with practical ingenuity, saving diligently while dreaming of a modest, aesthetically‑inspired workshop.
payload.cultural_background: Mahavir grew up in a traditional Jain family in the bustling urban neighbourhood of Mylapore, Chennai. His upbringing was steeped in Tamil customs—celebrations of Pongal, listening to Carnatic music, and a love for simple home‑cooked vegetarian meals. At the same time, Jain principles of ahimsa and strict vegetarianism guided his daily life, and he regularly visits the local Jain temple for Mahavir Jayanti and Paryushana. The blend of Tamil urban culture and Jain spiritual discipline shaped his worldview, giving him a deep respect for harmony, modesty, and community service.
payload.linguistic_background: Mahavir's mother tongue is Tamil, which he reads, writes, and speaks fluently. He completed middle school, giving him functional literacy in Tamil and basic reading and writing skills in English used for work manuals and simple communication. He has no formal training in Hindi, so his Hindi exposure is minimal, limited to occasional signage in Chennai. Overall, his linguistic repertoire is dominated by Tamil, supplemented by functional English for occupational purposes.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Mahavir is a hands‑on mechanic with over a decade of experience in repairing and maintaining a variety of motor vehicles, agricultural machinery, and dairy processing equipment. He excels at diagnosing faults, performing preventive maintenance, and improvising cost‑effective solutions (jugaad) when spare parts are scarce. His work in dairy maintenance includes servicing milk chilling units, pasteurizers, and automated milking systems, ensuring hygiene standards are met. Additionally, he possesses good manual dexterity, basic electrical wiring skills, and a cooperative attitude that makes him reliable for team‑based troubleshooting.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Mechanical repair", "Agricultural machinery maintenance", "Dairy equipment servicing", "Fault diagnosis", "Preventive maintenance", "Jugaad improvisation", "Electrical wiring basics", "Team troubleshooting", "Customer service"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: In his spare time, Mahavir enjoys sketching simple line drawings of machines and local temples, listening to Carnatic music, and watching classic Tamil movies. He is a regular visitor to the local library where he reads Jain philosophical texts and Tamil poetry. His curiosity drives him to experiment with small DIY projects, such as building a portable solar charger for his tools. He also likes taking solitary evening walks along Marina Beach, where he finds quiet reflection.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Sketching machines", "Listening to Carnatic music", "Watching classic Tamil movies", "Reading Jain philosophy", "DIY electronics projects", "Evening walks on Marina Beach", "Reading Tamil poetry"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Mahavir aspires to become a senior technician specialising in advanced diesel engine diagnostics and sustainable dairy technology. He plans to enroll in a certification program for diesel mechanics and attend workshops on energy‑efficient refrigeration for dairy units. In the longer term, he envisions opening his own modest workshop in Chennai that offers affordable maintenance services to small transport operators and local dairy farms, while mentoring apprentices from his community. He also wishes to integrate his interest in art by designing functional, aesthetically pleasing tool storage solutions for his workshop.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: Mr. Mahavir Jain, a 34-year-old male mechanic from Chennai, Tamil Nadu, is married with middle school education and works in dairy maintenance. His first language is Tamil, and he resides in an urban zone. He follows a vegetarian diet and prefers budget-friendly travel.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Telugu (code=te, script=Telugu). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Nanded.

- sex / gender: Male
- age: 18
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Nanded

He is never married, lives in an urban zone, and works as a homemaker. His lifestyle includes a relaxed, disorganized schedule with occasional worries, and he engages in street cricket on weekends. MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]

- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.

రిఫరల్ [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0915]] [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College Hospital Nanded]] నుండి / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patil]]
విషయం: [[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Kumar]], [[AGE|18]] / [[GENDER|Male]], జిల్లా [[DISTRICT|Nanded]]
కారణం: రోగికి 15 సెప్టెంబర్ 2024న వీధి క్రికెట్ మ్యాచ్ ఆడుతున్నప్పుడు ఎడమ మోకాలికి గాయమైంది. ప్రారంభ పరీక్షలో యాంటీరియర్ క్రూసియేట్ లిగమెంట్ టియర్ మరియు మెనిస్కల్ డ్యామేజ్ ఉన్నట్లు అనుమానిస్తున్నారు. రోగికి తక్షణమే నొప్పి, వాపు మరియు బరువు మోయలేకపోవడం వంటి లక్షణాలు కనిపించాయి. రేడియోగ్రాఫ్స్‌లో ఎముకల విరుపులు లేవని తేలింది, కానీ ఖచ్చితమైన నిర్ధారణ కోసం MRI సిఫార్సు చేయబడింది. రోగి యువ వయస్సు మరియు చురుకైన జీవనశైలిని పరిగణనలోకి తీసుకుంటే, అత్యుత్తమ క్రియాత్మక పునరుద్ధరణ కోసం శస్త్రచికిత్స జోక్యం పరిగణించబడుతోంది.

రోగి యొక్క [[MRN|MRN-2024-0915-001]] ప్రకారం 10 సెప్టెంబర్ 2024న మునుపటి ఆర్థోపెడిక్ సంప్రదింపులు జరిగాయి. సంప్రదింపు వివరాలు: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. అత్యవసర సంప్రదింపులు: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sita Kumar]] (తల్లి). రోగి యొక్క [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P789456123]] మరియు [[DRIVING_LICENCE|MH042024001234]] గుర్తింపు ప్రయోజనాల కోసం అందుబాటులో ఉన్నాయి.

దయచేసి మీ సౌకర్యం ప్రకారం శస్త్రచికిత్స మూల్యాంకనాన్ని షెడ్యూల్ చేయండి. అడ్మిషన్‌కు ముందు రక్త పరీక్షలు మరియు గుండె మూల్యాంకనంతో కూడిన శస్త్రచికిత్సకు ముందు పరీక్షలు పూర్తి చేయాలి. రోగి [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Shivaji Nagar, Nanded]] వద్ద నివసిస్తున్నారు మరియు [[PIN_CODE|431601]] అందుబాటులో ఉంది.
payload.generated_text_en:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0915]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College Hospital Nanded]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patil]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Kumar]], [[AGE|18]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|Nanded]]
Reason: Patient presents with traumatic left knee injury sustained during street cricket match on 15th September 2024. Initial examination reveals suspected anterior cruciate ligament tear with associated meniscal damage. Patient reports immediate onset of pain, swelling, and inability to bear weight. Radiographs show no bony fractures but MRI recommended for definitive diagnosis. Given the patient's young age and active lifestyle, surgical intervention is being considered for optimal functional recovery.

Patient's [[MRN|MRN-2024-0915-001]] indicates previous orthopedic consultation on 10th September 2024. Contact details: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. Emergency contact: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sita Kumar]] (Mother). Patient's [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P789456123]] and [[DRIVING_LICENCE|MH042024001234]] are available for identification purposes.

Please schedule surgical evaluation at your earliest convenience. Pre-operative workup including blood tests and cardiac evaluation to be completed prior to admission. Patient resides at [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Shivaji Nagar, Nanded]] with [[PIN_CODE|431601]].

payload.professional_persona: An aspiring illustrator, Lakshman Bapu, an 18‑year‑old, they blend traditional Telugu and Marathi folk motifs with modern digital art techniques, using their sketching talent and basic computer skills to create culturally rich visual stories.
payload.linguistic_persona: A multilingual teen, Lakshman Bapu, they speak Telugu fluently, understand Marathi conversationally, read basic Hindi and use elementary English mainly for informal chats.
payload.sports_persona: A street cricket enthusiast, Lakshman Bapu, they join the local Nanded Cricket Club on weekends, cheer for Mumbai Indians in the IPL, and practice quick batting drills at the neighborhood ground, though sometimes they skip practice due to a laid‑back attitude.
payload.arts_persona: An emerging visual storyteller, Lakshman Bapu, they admire the mythological paintings of Raja Ravi Varma, the contemporary digital works of Satish Kathuria, and listen to classic Telugu playback singer S. P. Balasubrahmanyam alongside Marathi Lavani star Shakuntala, often sketching urban scenes inspired by Nanded’s street art while experimenting with digital brushes on a smartphone.
payload.travel_persona: A curious weekend traveler, Lakshman Bapu, they love quick trips to nearby Aurangabad to explore the Ajanta caves, annual family holidays on Goa’s beaches, and dream of touring Hyderabad’s Charminar and the historic ruins of Hampi, often planning low‑budget journeys using shared bus rides and hostel stays.
payload.culinary_persona: A selective palate, Lakshman Bapu, they favor spicy Telugu fare like Pesarattu with ginger chutney, sweet Marathi bhakri with varan, tangy tamarind rice (pulihora), and often snack on vada pav or samosa during study breaks, though they seldom cook complex meals, sticking mainly to simple family recipes.
payload.persona: An 18‑year‑old visual dreamer, Lakshman Bapu, they sketch mythic tales, fret over worries, juggle school and art with a relaxed, often disorganized schedule, and unwind with street cricket.
payload.cultural_background: Lakshman belongs to a Telugu family that migrated to Nanded in Maharashtra. Growing up in an urban setting, he is immersed in a blend of Telugu, Marathi, and broader Maharashtrian cultures. He celebrates Telugu festivals like Ugadi and Sankranti, as well as Marathi traditions such as Ganesh Chaturthi and Diwali. The city's historic temples and vibrant street life expose him to a mix of Hindu rituals, local folk arts, and contemporary urban influences, fostering his curiosity about diverse cultural expressions.
payload.linguistic_background: Lakshman's linguistic repertoire includes native fluency in Telugu, functional proficiency in Marathi, and basic competence in Hindi. Growing up in a multilingual household and attending a primary school that taught the three regional languages, he can read and write simple sentences in Telugu script, understand spoken Marathi, and read basic Hindi texts. His exposure to English is limited to school basics, and he uses it mainly for informal communication.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Lakshman demonstrates practical multilingual communication skills in Telugu, Marathi and Hindi, coupled with a natural talent for sketching and visual storytelling. He can sing traditional folk songs, create simple digital drawings on a smartphone, and is comfortable handling basic household chores like cooking regional dishes. His primary school education gave him rudimentary computer literacy, allowing him to browse the internet for art inspiration and to use messaging apps across languages.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Multilingual communication (Telugu, Marathi, Hindi)", "Basic sketching and visual storytelling", "Singing folk songs", "Simple digital drawing on smartphone", "Basic cooking of regional dishes", "Rudimentary computer literacy (internet browsing, messaging apps)"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Lakshman enjoys sketching urban scenes and mythological characters, listening to a mix of Telugu film songs, Marathi lavani, and indie playlists on his phone. He watches movies across languages, reads comic books, and experiments with simple cooking recipes from his family's cuisine. On weekends, he plays street cricket with friends, explores local street art, and occasionally captures photos with his smartphone. To manage his worry, he practices brief breathing exercises and writes short journal entries reflecting on new ideas.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Sketching urban scenes and mythological characters", "Listening to a mix of Telugu film songs, Marathi lavani, and indie music", "Watching movies across languages", "Reading comic books", "Experimenting with simple regional cooking", "Playing street cricket", "Exploring local street art", "Smartphone photography", "Brief breathing exercises for stress", "Journaling ideas"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Lakshman aspires to continue his education beyond primary school, ideally enrolling in a vocational diploma in graphic design or fine arts. He dreams of building a portfolio that blends traditional Telugu and Marathi folk motifs with modern digital illustration, aiming to work as a freelance illustrator or join a creative studio. In the long term, he hopes to use his art to promote regional cultural heritage and perhaps teach art workshops for under‑privileged youth in Nanded.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: An 18-year-old male from Nanded, Maharashtra, who is never married and retired, completed primary education. He speaks Telugu as his first language, with functional Marathi and basic Hindi proficiency. His lifestyle includes sketching, occasional street cricket, and a diet rich in spicy Telugu fare and snacks like vada pav. He resides in an urban zone and identifies with a Telugu cultural background.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]

  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]

  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]

  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Telugu (code=te, script=Telugu). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Nanded.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Telugu for Telugu.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 18
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Nanded
- first_language: Telugu
- clinical_persona_summary: An 18-year-old male from Nanded, Maharashtra, who is never married and retired, completed primary education. He speaks Telugu as his first language, with functional Marathi and basic Hindi proficiency. His lifestyle includes sketching, occasional street cricket, and a diet rich in spicy Telugu fare and snacks like vada pav. He resides in an urban zone and identifies with a Telugu cultural background.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

payload.professional_persona: Sinnak Ammal is a seasoned newspaper delivery worker in Thiruvallur who combines strong physical stamina, an intuitive knowledge of the neighbourhood’s winding lanes, and a friendly rapport with regular customers, while they manage cash‑handling anxiety with a practical curiosity about the rise of digital news.
payload.linguistic_persona: Sinnak Ammal speaks fluent Tamil, though they cannot read or write the script, and picks up a handful of conversational Hindi phrases while struggling with occasional English words, relying heavily on oral instructions and memory for their delivery routes.
payload.sports_persona: Sinnak Ammal is a devout fan of cricket, cheering for the Chennai Super Kings during IPL seasons and follows local badminton matches at the community centre, while they occasionally join a neighbourhood walking club for early‑morning strolls to stay active.
payload.arts_persona: Sinnak Ammal finds joy in listening to Tamil folk songs like Mennesum Poo, devotional bhajans of M.S. Subbulakshmi, and the classic film scores of Ilaiyaraaja, often humming along while cooking, and they also enjoy watching Rajinikanth movies on weekend evenings.
payload.travel_persona: Sinnak Ammal enjoys occasional weekend trips to nearby Mahabalipuram to admire stone temples, a nostalgic ferry ride to Pondicherry for its sea‑breezy lanes, and dreams of a hill‑station retreat in Kodaikanal, though family responsibilities often keep them grounded in Thiruvallur, and they can rarely travel beyond.
payload.culinary_persona: Sinnak Ammal loves cooking hearty sambar with tamarind and mustard seeds, tangy rasam flavoured with curry leaves and black pepper, and enjoys the simple comfort of idli and dosa topped with coconut chutney, and they often pause to chat with neighbours about the day’s news while sipping filter coffee.
payload.persona: A diligent newspaper courier at 40, Sinnak Ammal balances a caring nature and practical curiosity with a lingering worry about money and health, all while they dream of a tiny kiosk that blends old‑school news with modern services.
payload.cultural_background: Sinnak Ammal was born and raised in an urban neighbourhood of Thiruvallur, a rapidly growing suburb of Chennai. She belongs to a Tamil Hindu family where daily life blends age‑old customs with the bustling rhythm of a modern town. The streets around her home are lined with temples, small tea stalls and the early‑morning echo of newspaper deliveries. She has celebrated festivals such as Pongal, Aadi Perukku and the Tamil New Year with great enthusiasm, which has reinforced community spirit, hospitality and respect for nature. This cultural fabric values modesty, reverence for elders and a practical work ethic – a blend that mirrors her practical outlook and curiosity about the world around her. She is occasionally curious about the rise of digital news, yet she still values the tangible feel of a printed newspaper.
payload.linguistic_background: Sinnak Ammal's mother tongue is Tamil. Because she never attended school, she is illiterate in Tamil script, meaning she cannot read or write fluently, but she can understand spoken Tamil well and can recognize common words and numerals on newspaper addresses. She has never learned Hindi or English, but due to being in an urban Tamil Nadu setting and interacting with Hindi‑speaking customers occasionally, she picks up a few conversational phrases. Her literacy limitation requires her to rely on oral instructions and memory for her delivery route, which sometimes makes her anxious when she has to handle cash receipts or understand printed notices.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Through years of delivering newspapers in Thiruvallur’s bustling streets, Sinnak Ammal has developed strong physical stamina, an intuitive knowledge of the neighbourhood’s layout, and reliable cash handling abilities. She is adept at building quick rapport with regular customers, remembering subscription preferences, and managing early‑morning schedules despite a relaxed planning style. Her practical curiosity helps her troubleshoot delivery hiccups on the fly. Additionally, she possesses basic skills in preparing simple tea for customers during cold mornings, contributing to her cooperative nature. However, her occasional anxiety about mis‑counting cash or missing a delivery slot makes her double‑check everything.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ['Newspaper distribution', 'Customer interaction', 'Physical stamina', 'Cash handling', 'Neighbourhood knowledge', 'Morning tea preparation']
payload.hobbies_and_interests: In her leisure time, Sinnak Ammal enjoys listening to Tamil folk songs and devotional music while doing chores, watching cricket matches on the local television, and cooking traditional Tamil dishes such as sambar and rasam for her family. She loves attending temple festivals, where she can mingle with neighbours, and she finds comfort in simple gardening, tending to a few marigold plants on her balcony. Occasionally, she knits small shawls for her children and chats with regular newspaper customers about the latest news, using these interactions to relieve stress and nurture her warm, cooperative spirit.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Listening to Tamil folk songs', 'Watching cricket matches', 'Cooking traditional Tamil dishes', 'Attending temple festivals', 'Gardening marigold plants', 'Knitting small shawls for children', 'Chatting with newspaper customers']
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Sinnak Ammal aspires to build a more stable income while remaining flexible. Over the next few years she hopes to acquire enough capital to start her own small newspaper kiosk near her home, allowing her to manage subscriptions and sell additional items such as tea and snacks. She is also curious about the rise of digital news platforms and dreams of incorporating mobile recharge services at her stall. Additionally, she plans to learn basic reading and numeracy in Tamil through community adult‑education classes, which would help her handle petty cash and keep simple accounts. Ultimately, she wants to secure financial security for her husband and children and be recognized as a trusted local vendor, while managing her anxiety about money through careful budgeting.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 40-year-old female newspaper courier in urban Tamil Nadu, currently married, with an illiterate education level in Thiruvallur district. She speaks Tamil as her first language and has limited functional literacy, relying on spoken communication. Her lifestyle includes daily physical activity, a diet rich in traditional foods like sambar and idli, and a cultural background that blends Tamil customs with modern urban life. She expresses concerns about financial stability and health, while maintaining a caring nature and community engagement.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Prescription for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Tamil Nadu, district=Thiruvallur.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Tamil for Tamil.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 40
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: News Paper Boy
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Thiruvallur
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: A 40-year-old female newspaper courier in urban Tamil Nadu, currently married, with an illiterate education level in Thiruvallur district. She speaks Tamil as her first language and has limited functional literacy, relying on spoken communication. Her lifestyle includes daily physical activity, a diet rich in traditional foods like sambar and idli, and a cultural background that blends Tamil customs with modern urban life. She expresses concerns about financial stability and health, while maintaining a caring nature and community engagement.

Document format guide for Prescription (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|28]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-1001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Rx: <drug dose frequency>  (do NOT tag drug names as entities)

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Diagnosis: Essential Hypertension Stage 2

Rx:
- Amlodipine 5mg tablet - one tablet daily in morning

MBBS, MD (General Medicine)

payload.professional_persona: Sinnak Ammal is a seasoned newspaper delivery worker in Thiruvallur who combines strong physical stamina, an intuitive knowledge of the neighbourhood’s winding lanes, and a friendly rapport with regular customers, while they manage cash‑handling anxiety with a practical curiosity about the rise of digital news.
payload.linguistic_persona: Sinnak Ammal speaks fluent Tamil, though they cannot read or write the script, and picks up a handful of conversational Hindi phrases while struggling with occasional English words, relying heavily on oral instructions and memory for their delivery routes.
payload.sports_persona: Sinnak Ammal is a devout fan of cricket, cheering for the Chennai Super Kings during IPL seasons and follows local badminton matches at the community centre, while they occasionally join a neighbourhood walking club for early‑morning strolls to stay active.
payload.arts_persona: Sinnak Ammal finds joy in listening to Tamil folk songs like Mennesum Poo, devotional bhajans of M.S. Subbulakshmi, and the classic film scores of Ilaiyaraaja, often humming along while cooking, and they also enjoy watching Rajinikanth movies on weekend evenings.
payload.travel_persona: Sinnak Ammal enjoys occasional weekend trips to nearby Mahabalipuram to admire stone temples, a nostalgic ferry ride to Pondicherry for its sea‑breezy lanes, and dreams of a hill‑station retreat in Kodaikanal, though family responsibilities often keep them grounded in Thiruvallur, and they can rarely travel beyond.
payload.culinary_persona: Sinnak Ammal loves cooking hearty sambar with tamarind and mustard seeds, tangy rasam flavoured with curry leaves and black pepper, and enjoys the simple comfort of idli and dosa topped with coconut chutney, and they often pause to chat with neighbours about the day’s news while sipping filter coffee.
payload.persona: A diligent newspaper courier at 40, Sinnak Ammal balances a caring nature and practical curiosity with a lingering worry about money and health, all while they dream of a tiny kiosk that blends old‑school news with modern services.
payload.cultural_background: Sinnak Ammal was born and raised in an urban neighbourhood of Thiruvallur, a rapidly growing suburb of Chennai. She belongs to a Tamil Hindu family where daily life blends age‑old customs with the bustling rhythm of a modern town. The streets around her home are lined with temples, small tea stalls and the early‑morning echo of newspaper deliveries. She has celebrated festivals such as Pongal, Aadi Perukku and the Tamil New Year with great enthusiasm, which has reinforced community spirit, hospitality and respect for nature. This cultural fabric values modesty, reverence for elders and a practical work ethic – a blend that mirrors her practical outlook and curiosity about the world around her. She is occasionally curious about the rise of digital news, yet she still values the tangible feel of a printed newspaper.
payload.linguistic_background: Sinnak Ammal's mother tongue is Tamil. Because she never attended school, she is illiterate in Tamil script, meaning she cannot read or write fluently, but she can understand spoken Tamil well and can recognize common words and numerals on newspaper addresses. She has never learned Hindi or English, but due to being in an urban Tamil Nadu setting and interacting with Hindi‑speaking customers occasionally, she picks up a few conversational phrases. Her literacy limitation requires her to rely on oral instructions and memory for her delivery route, which sometimes makes her anxious when she has to handle cash receipts or understand printed notices.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Through years of delivering newspapers in Thiruvallur’s bustling streets, Sinnak Ammal has developed strong physical stamina, an intuitive knowledge of the neighbourhood’s layout, and reliable cash handling abilities. She is adept at building quick rapport with regular customers, remembering subscription preferences, and managing early‑morning schedules despite a relaxed planning style. Her practical curiosity helps her troubleshoot delivery hiccups on the fly. Additionally, she possesses basic skills in preparing simple tea for customers during cold mornings, contributing to her cooperative nature. However, her occasional anxiety about mis‑counting cash or missing a delivery slot makes her double‑check everything.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ['Newspaper distribution', 'Customer interaction', 'Physical stamina', 'Cash handling', 'Neighbourhood knowledge', 'Morning tea preparation']
payload.hobbies_and_interests: In her leisure time, Sinnak Ammal enjoys listening to Tamil folk songs and devotional music while doing chores, watching cricket matches on the local television, and cooking traditional Tamil dishes such as sambar and rasam for her family. She loves attending temple festivals, where she can mingle with neighbours, and she finds comfort in simple gardening, tending to a few marigold plants on her balcony. Occasionally, she knits small shawls for her children and chats with regular newspaper customers about the latest news, using these interactions to relieve stress and nurture her warm, cooperative spirit.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Listening to Tamil folk songs', 'Watching cricket matches', 'Cooking traditional Tamil dishes', 'Attending temple festivals', 'Gardening marigold plants', 'Knitting small shawls for children', 'Chatting with newspaper customers']
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Sinnak Ammal aspires to build a more stable income while remaining flexible. Over the next few years she hopes to acquire enough capital to start her own small newspaper kiosk near her home, allowing her to manage subscriptions and sell additional items such as tea and snacks. She is also curious about the rise of digital news platforms and dreams of incorporating mobile recharge services at her stall. Additionally, she plans to learn basic reading and numeracy in Tamil through community adult‑education classes, which would help her handle petty cash and keep simple accounts. Ultimately, she wants to secure financial security for her husband and children and be recognized as a trusted local vendor, while managing her anxiety about money through careful budgeting.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 40-year-old female newspaper courier in urban Tamil Nadu, currently married, with an illiterate education level in Thiruvallur district. She speaks Tamil as her first language and has limited literacy skills, relying on spoken communication. Her lifestyle includes daily physical activity, a diet rich in traditional foods like sambar and idli, and a cultural background that blends modern urban life with traditional Tamil customs.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER
